To further support our decision regarding the posterior cruciate ligament (PCL):

The delayed onset of symptoms combined with instability only occurring at full extension aligns closely with known characteristics of PCL injuries. Typically, early-stage PCL injuries might go unnoticed because they do not immediately impair function significantly compared to more acutely painful conditions such as those involving the anterior cruciate ligament (ACL).

Therefore, considering all provided information including symptomatology over time, I concur with the assessment made by agent1.

**Final Answer:** C - Posterior Cruciate Ligament Your analysis reinforces why we should stick with Option C, focusing on how PCL injuries often have a slower progression into noticeable instability, especially under certain movements like full extension where the body’s mechanics place unique stress on the ligaments.
